Av. Dr. Cardoso de Melo, 878 - 5º andar - cjto - 51 - Vila Olímpia
São Paulo SP 04548-003
Diana serves as the Managing Partner of Fragomen in Brazil, where she has played a pivotal role in establishing and growing the firm’s operations across the country. Today, Fragomen Brazil serves a diverse array of multinational corporations and local branches of global companies in various industries, positioning itself as a trusted partner for immigration needs under Diana’s leadership.
Diana oversees a dedicated team of more than 50 professionals across Fragomen’s São Paulo and Rio de Janeiro offices, ensuring that the Brazil team delivers tailored solutions for its wide-ranging client base. Her client service approach emphasizes the importance of strong, lasting relationships and personalized care, which has fostered an environment of trust and collaboration. She is known for her strategic insights and her ability to work closely with authorities to expedite visa processes and resolve complex issues, often securing quicker outcomes for clients facing bureaucratic delays.
In addition to leading Fragomen’s Brazil offices, Diana is the Vice President of the Brazilian Migration Association (ABEMMI), an organization she co-founded in 2017. The ABEMMI was created in response to major changes in Brazil’s migration laws and Diana has served on its board since its inception. The association has grown significantly since its founding, now comprising 76 member organizations, including immigration firms, law firms, tax companies and multinational corporations. Diana plays an active role in advocating for policies that support the industry, working closely with government officials to propose and influence legislation. Her efforts have earned the ABEMMI a reputation as a leading voice in Brazil’s migration landscape.
Diana’s experience has made her a highly sought-after thought leader in the media, frequently offering her insights on Brazil’s immigration policies and global mobility trends. Her work, especially in the oil and gas sector, for example, where she handles the specific immigration needs of offshore crews, has earned her the respect of top-tier industry organizations.
A passionate advocate for social causes, Diana has also led initiatives to help local communities in need, such as during the 2024 floods and landslides in southern Brazil. She helped to organize Fragomen employee donations and support efforts, demonstrating her commitment to making a positive impact in addition to her professional work.
Diana speaks English, Portuguese and Spanish.

- University of Oxford, Oxford Program on Negotiation, 2012
- Fundação Getúlio Vargas, Specialization in Business Administration, 2006
- Universidade Braz Cubas, Licentiate Degree, Attorney at Law, 2003
Bar Admissions: Brazil

### Professional accomplishments
Member, The Law Society, Brazil
Vice President, Associação Brasileira de Especialistas em Migração e Mobilidade Internacional (ABEMMI) Executive Committee
Director of the Board, ABEMMI
Was responsible for supervising a team of 40 professionals in São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Campinas, Brasilia, Belo Horizonte and Porto Alegre
Represented multinational companies with respect to all areas associated with an expatriate assignment in Brazil

### Publications and lectures
Speaker, “ Brazil: Early Career Visas .” Fragomen , Webinar, 24 February 2026.
Speaker, “ Important Update for Brazil-Bound Visitors .” Fragomen Mobility Minute , Podcast, 10 October 2025.
Quoted, “ Brasil volta a exigir vistos de turista a cidadãos dos EUA, do Canadá e da Austrália .” Publico , 4 April 2025.
Quoted, “ A nova política de imigração dos EUA pode afetar turistas brasileiros? Veja o que dizem especialistas em vistos .” O Globo , 2 March 2025.
Speaker, “ Fragomen Webcast: Latin America Immigration Trends and Key Themes of Q2 and Q3 2021 .” Fragomen , 9 September 2021.

## Explore more at Fragomen
Fragomen earned top national and regional rankings in Chambers USA 2026, including its 13th consecutive year in Band 1 nationwide.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold examines the remaining opportunities under Ireland’s winding-down Immigrant Investor Programme and what they mean for eligible investors and their families.
Government Affairs Strategy Director Shuyeb Muquit analyses the UK’s latest net migration decline, exploring what the data reveals about policy impact, economic trade‑offs and the risks of over‑correction in future migration strategy.
Partner Bo Cooper discusses the practical considerations facing employers and foreign nationals amid recent changes to green card processing.
In this #FragomenFC episode, Partner Rick Lamanna, Senior Manager Sergio Flores and Senior Associate Jake Paul Minster discuss final travel and immigration considerations ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Senior Manager Samantha Arnold discusses Ireland’s citizenship framework and the gaps that can remain following reforms to birthright citizenship.
In this video, Partner Diana Quintas discusses how Brazil’s family reunion visa supports long-term international assignments and outlines key eligibility, documentation and compliance considerations for employers and families relocating to Brazil.
Attorney María José Clarke explores Chile’s intensified immigration enforcement framework under President José Antonio Kast, examining new employer compliance obligations, work authorization rules for foreign nationals and the legal and financial consequences of unauthorized employment.
Senior Manager Jonathan Hill examines how the UK’s new RAG rating system and stricter compliance metrics could shape universities’ approaches to international student recruitment and sponsorship compliance.
Knowledge Management Director Ana Sofia Walsh and Senior Client Engagement Manager Soraya Driessen examine the European Commission’s EU Inc. proposal and its potential to reshape corporate structures across the EU, highlighting the resulting complexities and unresolved questions for immigration, work authorization and cross-border workforce mobility.
